title: Morgan Wallen Debuts ‘Been By Now,’ His Next Single, Live in Baltimore
summary: The country hitmaker announced the song will be released Friday (July 24).
body: Morgan Wallen revealed the title and release date of his next single on Saturday night (July 18), giving fans the first listen of “Been By Now.” Related Morgan Wallen, Garth Brooks, Taylor Swift & More Artists With Most Weeks at No. 1 on Top Country Albums Chart: Full List How Similar Was the World Cup Halftime Show to the Super Bowl? Coldplay's 'We Dance' Closes 2026 World Cup Halftime Show With The Muppets, PS22 Chorus & More Wallen played the song live for the first time at his show in Baltimore, Md., at M&T Bank Stadium and revealed it’ll officially be released on Friday (July 24), as seen in videos filmed by concertgoers. He’s currently on his Still the Problem Tour, which brings him to Ann Arbor, Mich., next weekend and then wraps in Philadelphia, Pa. Post-show, he shared a 40-second clip of the recorded version of “Been By Now” on social media . “If it was meant to be, we’d have been by now/ We wouldn’t have to keep trying to work things out,” Wallen sings on the midtempo track that seems to be about a relationship that isn’t progressing, even though “baby, we’re a …

title: Morgan Wallen, Garth Brooks, Taylor Swift & More Artists With Most Weeks at No. 1 on Top Country Albums Chart: Full List
summary: Male solo artists dominate, though three female solo artists and two groups have also achieved the feat.
body: Morgan Wallen ’s I’m the Problem leads Billboard ‘s Top Country Albums chart (dated July 25) for the 45th nonconsecutive week. This ups Wallen’s career tally of weeks at No. 1 to 231, allowing him to extend his lead as the artist with the most weeks at No. 1 in the chart’s 62-year history. Wallen’s achievement is especially impressive because he has amassed this hefty total of weeks at No. 1 in such a short amount of time. He first topped the chart on Aug. 15, 2020. Before returning to No. 1 this week, I’m the Problem spent the past 13 weeks at No. 2 behind Ella Langley ‘s Dandelion . That was the longest Wallen has been locked out of the top spot since he rose to superstar status in January 2021 with Dangerous: The Double Album . Top Country Albums originated as Hot Country Albums in the Jan. 11, 1964 issue of Billboard . Johnny Cash ’s Ring of Fire (The Best of Johnny Cash) headed the inaugural chart. The compilation was well-timed: “Ring of Fire” had headed Hot Country Songs for seven weeks the previous summer and had crossed over to reach the top 20 on the Billboard Hot 100. The chart was named Hot Country LP’s from 1968 to 1984, when vinyl …
